mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and aggregate supply, which historically translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return during periods of INDPRO month-over-month increase is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4%, the labor market enters extreme tightness that historically precedes Federal Reserve tightening cycles and subsequent equity market declines over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:historical Fed tightening after sub-4% unemployment
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return following UNRATE < 4.0 is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the signal does not underperform a no-trigger benchmark over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate combined with elevated consumer price inflation signals that the central bank is tightening policy to combat persistent price pressures, which depresses equity valuations via higher discount rates over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a linear model does not have a statistically significant negative coefficient (p < 0.05) for 60-day forward returns, or the Rank IC of the combined signal remains below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During the recovery phase after an economic contraction, positive real GDP growth signals that the economy has exited recession and is expanding, which historically drives strong equity performance as corporate fundamentals improve over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:post-recession recovery periods show positive equity returns
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return in quarters following NBER-defined recession troughs is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or the Sharpe ratio in walk-forward tests is below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed a robust annualized rate of 1.5 million units, it signals strong residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to positive short-term equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return following HOUST > 1500 is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the signal's average excess return over no-trigger periods is less than 0.5% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent increases in the consumer price index erode real purchasing power and force the Federal Reserve to maintain or tighten monetary policy, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return during periods of month-over-month CPIAUCSL increase is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window is above -0.03 (i.e., not reliably negative).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in payroll employment and decreases in the unemployment rate indicate exceptionally tight labor market conditions that historically lead to wage inflation pressures, prompting Fed tightening and resulting in negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal (interaction in a linear model) does not have a statistically significant negative coefficient (p < 0.05) for 20-day forward returns, or the combined signal's Rank IC over any 2-year window is above -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production and retail sales rise concurrently, it signals broad-based demand growth matched by supply expansion, a favorable combination for corporate earnings and short-term equity gains.
failure_condition: The joint signal (interaction in a linear model) does not have a statistically significant positive coefficient (p < 0.05) for 5-day forward returns, or the combined signal's Rank IC over any 1-year rolling window is below 0.03.
